name: Potential Empire Activity
notes: This rule detects Empire activity which hijacks Microsoft Outlook to exfiltrate data over mail.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Information, Audit.Object Load Success
when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES '.*?84da0a92-25e0-11d3-b9f7-00c04f4c8f5d.*') OR LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES '.*?84da0a92-25e0-11d3-b9f7-00c04f4c8f5d.*') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Empire Activity on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Information, Audit.Object Load Success

AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES '.*?84da0a92-25e0-11d3-b9f7-00c04f4c8f5d.*') OR LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES '.*?84da0a92-25e0-11d3-b9f7-00c04f4c8f5d.*') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects Empire activity which hijacks Microsoft Outlook to exfiltrate data over mail.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
